behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: for a multi-speaker meeting audio, the transcript consumed by summarization must retain correct speaker labels per utterance. This failure depends on real speech-model diarization/output quality (LLM-DEP), which stubs can hide. System concerns (real HTTP) exist, but the behavior’s core correctness is model-output dependent, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets a single model-output property (speaker-labeled transcript), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real STT API call on a known multi-speaker recording with ground-truth speaker turns; assert the returned transcript preserves speaker labels correctly enough for downstream attendee attribution input.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: given a transcript sentence with an explicit assignment, parser output must include both assignee and task text fields. The behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic within a local boundary (MOCK-MISS: no required cross-module failure boundary in the behavior text). No real infra or model output is required.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test parser function with representative transcript lines (including edge punctuation and names) and assert exact structured extraction of assignee and task text.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: every attendee assigned a task in input context appears with at least one action item in output summary. This depends on real summarizer model decision quality; deterministic mocks would always pass and hide omission failures (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow: the invariant can be exposed with one real summarization step from prepared transcript/context, without requiring multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a real summarization model a transcript (or prepared summary input) with explicit assignments across multiple attendees; assert output action_items covers each assigned attendee at least once.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: when input contains already-identified decisions, formatter/router places them in `decisions` schema field and not narrative field. This is local transformation/routing logic; real infra and real model output are not required for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test routing function with input containing identified decisions plus narrative text; assert decisions land only in `decisions` and are excluded from generic narrative slots.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: key decisions present in transcript must be surfaced in output `decisions` section. This is output-quality dependence on real summarizer/extractor model behavior; stubs cannot validate whether the model actually elevates decisions versus burying them (LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow: behavior can be tested with a single real model pass over transcript for this criterion.
behaviors.test_description: Run real model summarization on transcripts with clear decision statements; assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: for >60-minute audio, segment-by-segment processing and merge must produce one coherent final summary preserving cross-segment content. This behavior explicitly chains multiple processing steps and multiple model calls (MULTI-STEP), so minimum boundary is Workflow. Integration/System alone would miss coherence failures caused by sequential agentic outputs and merge interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real long-audio pipeline path on a >60-minute meeting recording, verify segment sequencing occurs, and evaluate merged final summary for cross-segment coherence and completeness of major outcomes.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: a real recording can traverse upload → transcription → summarization → structured delivery automatically end-to-end. This is an explicit full pipeline with multiple agentic stages plus real infrastructure boundaries; minimum is Workflow, not merely System, because correctness requires sequenced model-driven stages to succeed together.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with a real meeting audio file in production-like environment, asserting unattended completion, successful handoffs across all stages, and valid structured output delivery.
